title: Navtej Singh Johar v. Union of India
legal_principles: Right to privacy includes sexual orientation
State cannot criminalise consensual adult behaviour
summary: Unanimously decriminalised consensual same-sex relations among adults by reading down Section 377 IPC. Overruled Suresh Kumar Koushal (2013) which had reinstated Section 377. The Court held that criminalising consensual adult same-sex conduct violates Articles 14, 15, 19, and 21.
key_holdings: Section 377 IPC unconstitutional to the extent it criminalises consensual adult same-sex conduct
Sexual orientation is a protected ground under Article 15 (discrimination)
Right to privacy includes right to one's sexual orientation
Criminalising consensual adult relationships violates dignity and autonomy under Article 21
key_quotes: An individual's sexual orientation is a natural phenomenon.
History owes an apology to members of the LGBT community.
why_important: Landmark judgment decriminalising homosexuality in India. Affirmed dignity and autonomy as core constitutional values.
relevance_to_legal_finance_system: Anti-discrimination compliance obligations for employers now extend to sexual orientation under evolving law.
